title: The Cup He Came to Drink
subtitle: On the road, going up to Jerusalem, He tells the Twelve in secret what awaits Him

stillness: Be still, for the road goes up to Jerusalem.
The precious treasure is enclosed in the better vessels.
Secret mysteries are revealed only to those drawn close.
Come apart, then, and be among His own.
gospel: They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem,
and Jesus was going before them.
And they were amazed, and following Him they were afraid.
And taking the Twelve apart, He began to tell them what would befall Him:
Behold, we go up to Jerusalem,
and all things shall be accomplished which were written by the Prophets concerning the Son of Man.
For He shall be delivered to the chief priests and the Scribes and the elders,
and they shall condemn Him to death,
and shall deliver Him to the Gentiles,
and they shall mock Him, and spit upon Him,
and scourge Him, and put Him to death;
and the third day He shall rise again.
behold: Behold the place of the Passion, which is Jerusalem;
for, according to the Scriptures, He was to work salvation in the midst of the earth.
He went before them, that He might show He did not flee death, but ran ahead to the Passion for our salvation.
And turning aside from the way, He took the Twelve apart, in secret, as the more perfect ones,
and to them alone He announced the mystery of His death.
Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, He says — and here appears the will to suffer, for of His own accord He comes to the Passion.
See that I go willingly to death: no one calls Me to Jerusalem, no one compels Me, I fear the command of no power.
He goes before, leading us, instructing us, that we should follow Him in the works of penance.
consider: He told them His Passion many times, that the foreknown tribulation might be found lighter, and they not be greatly troubled.
For tribulation foreseen and awaited is borne more easily.
He named Himself the Son of Man where He foretold His suffering, that He might be understood to suffer according to His human nature, not the divine.
If to be able to die belongs to man, yet to will to die does not belong to man.
Now He sets the whole order of the Passion in a row before them.
He shall be delivered by Judas to the chief priests and Scribes and elders;
they shall condemn Him to death by false witnesses, no order of law observed.
And the Gentiles shall mock Him with words and insults, and spit upon Him with the filth of their mouths,
and scourge Him with rods and knots, and put Him to death, nailing Him with iron nails.
Outrage beyond measure: where the false betray the Truth, fools mock Wisdom,
the guilty scourge Innocence, the wretched spit upon Glory, the dead slay Life.
Alas, dearest brothers! the freedom of captives is handed over;
the spotless mirror and the brightness of eternal light is spat upon;
the Lord of all is scourged, and the life of men is slain.
What remains, but that we all go, and die with Him?
Mystically, He foretold His Passion often, signifying that He would suffer many times in His members.
Saul, Saul, why do you persecute Me? — O how often, how greatly, the Lord suffers today in His elect!
prayer: who for our salvation willed to drink the cup of the Passion,
and to be drenched in the baptism of Your blood,
and came not to rule and be ministered to in worldly fashion,
but humbly to serve and to minister,
and to give Your life for our redemption:
grant to me, a wretched one, according to my small measure,
to become a partaker of Your cup and Your baptism, with patience and joy of spirit,
and to imitate You, my Master and Lord, in the works of humility,
and not only to spend my ministry on my neighbors in works of mercy,
but even to lay down my life for them, if it be necessary,
and at last to come happily to the heavenly kingdom prepared for Your elect.

latinJewel.latin: Injuria nimia ubi falsi tradunt veritatem, fatui illudunt sapientiam, rei flagellant innocentiam, miseri conspuunt gloriam, mortui occidunt vitam.
latinJewel.english: Outrage beyond measure: where the false betray the Truth, fools mock Wisdom, the guilty scourge Innocence, the wretched spit upon Glory, the dead slay Life.
